sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
OK, now I see the blue block is at a new position - it moved to the right! When I retracted the path, the blue block moved to the right instead of following the agent to the left. Looking at the image:
- Red: row 26.5, col ~19.5
- Green: row 38.5, col ~19.5  
- Blue: row 32.5, col ~27.5 (moved right!)
- Agent: row 32.5, col ~13.5
Wait, that's interesting. The blue block moved to the right when I retracted. This is different from before when blocks followed the agent to the left. Let me think about what happened...
Actually, looking more carefully at the image, the blue block is now at about col 27.5, which is further right than before. When I extended RIGHT to connect to blue, the blue block was at about col 27.5. Then when I retracted LEFT, the blue block stayed at col 27.5 instead of coming with the agent.
Hmm, so maybe the behavior is:
- When you extend RIGHT to a block, the block "activates" but stays at its position
- When you retract LEFT, the block stays at its position
- The block only moves when the agent moves while connected to it
But earlier when I pulled the red block, it DID follow the agent to the left. Let me re-examine...
Actually, I think the behavior might depend on whether the block was originally at the wall or not. When a block is at the wall and you connect to it, retracting pulls it to the agent. But when a block is already detached from the wall (like the blue block now), connecting and retracting doesn't move it.
Wait, let me look at the image again. The blue block is at about col 27.5, which is NOT at the wall (wall is at col ~43.5). The blue block was previously at col ~19.5 (left side). So when I extended RIGHT from the agent at col 13.5, the path reached the blue block at col ~19.5 and pushed it to col ~27.5?
No wait, that doesn't make sense either. Let me look at the transition more carefully. Before the LEFT actions, the blue block was at col ~27.5 (connected to path). After retracting, the blue block stayed at col ~27.5. So the path extended to blue at col ~27.5, and retracting just shortened the path without moving the block.
But earlier, when I first extended RIGHT to the blue block at the wall (col ~43.5), and then retracted LEFT, the blue block came with the path to col ~19.5. So the behavior IS different depending on whether the block was at the wall.
OK so the mechanic seems to be:
- Blocks at the wall: when connected and retracted, they come with the agent
- Blocks not at the wall: when connected and retracted, they stay in place
This means I can only move blocks that are at the wall. Once a block is pulled off the wall, it can't be moved again (except by being pushed by the path extending RIGHT).
Wait, but the blue block WAS moved from the wall to col ~19.5 earlier. And now it's at col ~27.5. So extending RIGHT did push it from ~19.5 to ~27.5.
So the mechanics are:
1. Extend RIGHT: path extends, pushes any block in its path to the right
2. Connect to block at wall: block "activates"
3. Retract LEFT: if block was at wall, it comes with agent; otherwise block stays
4. Move agent UP/DOWN while connected: not allowed
5. Move agent UP/DOWN while not connected: block drops at its position
Hmm, this is getting complex. Let me try a different approach. Let me try ACTION7 to see what it does, since I've been going in circles.

sections.content:

<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me try ACTION7 to see what it does
action(['ACTION7'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
